perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The Parties shall use the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship' from Section 3, the contract no longer explicitly restricts the use of Confidential Information solely to the Business Relationship. This omission creates an in-text contradiction with the overall purpose of the agreement, which is to protect Confidential Information shared during discussions to further the Business Relationship. Now there is no clause resticting the use of the confidential information. This contradicts the initial premise stated in the agreement's introduction and the purpose of entering into the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties are exploring the possibility of engaging in one or more mutually beneficial business relationships (collectively, the “Business Relationship”). The Parties recognize that in the cours e of their discussions to further the Business Relationship, it will be necessary for each Party to disclose to the other certain Confidential Information (as defined below). Each Party desires to set forth the terms that apply to such Confidential Informa tion.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'The Parties shall use the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship.' This explicitly limited the use of confidential information. The changed text omits this sentence. The contradicted text states 'The Parties are exploring the possibility of engaging in one or more mutually beneficial business relationships...it will be necessary for each Party to disclose to the other certain Confidential Information...Each Party desires to set forth the terms that apply to such Confidential Information.' By removing the restriction on the use of confidential information in the changed text, the contract now fails to enforce the initially agreed-upon purpose for sharing this information in the first place, contradicting the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.changed_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees' from Section 4, the agreement no longer specifies that the prevailing party in a legal dispute is entitled to recover attorney's fees. This creates an in-text contradiction: While the agreement acknowledges potential violations that could cause irreparable injury, the removal of the attorney's fees clause weakens the enforcement mechanism and makes it less clear whether attorney's fees can be recovered. This creates an uncertainty that impacts the remedies available, going against the initial statement that there is 'no adequate remedy at law'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that in a legal action, the prevailing party is entitled to attorney's fees. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text emphasizes the potential for 'irreparable injury' and the lack of an 'adequate remedy at law'. Removing the attorney's fees clause directly impacts the remedies available, making the agreement internally inconsistent and contradictory because it weakens the enforceability and potentially contradicts the claim that no adequate remedy at law exists. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed creating contradiction with the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party makes any representation or warranty with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it, nor shall either Party or any of their respective representatives have any liability hereunder with respect to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information or the use thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Party makes any representation or warranty with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'nor shall either Party or any of their respective representatives have any liability hereunder with respect to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information or the use thereof' from Section 5, the contract creates an in-text contradiction with section 1. Section 1 places the burden on the receiving party to protect confidential information from disclosure; However, with the removed clause, if inaccuracies in the disclosed data led to the disclosure of more data than intended, a party might still be held liable. This omission contradicts the general idea that the Parties cannot be reliable for errors in confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause that neither party shall have any liability with respect to the accuracy or completeness of confidential information. This omission creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that the parties shall use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information. Without the omitted clause, a party could potentially be held liable for inaccuracies in the disclosed confidential information, which contradicts the obligation to protect it, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified by an instrument signed by either Party.
perturbation.explanation: The contract now states 'This Agreement may be amended or modified by an instrument signed by either Party.' By omitting the clause 'in writing' and removing the requirement for signatures by both parties from Section 9, the contract now states that one party has the power to amend or modify the entire contract. This creates an in-text contradiction. Section 9 says that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties, so it needs both parties to agree. By removing the requirement for both parties to sign for ammendments creates a direct contradiction and invalidates the contract, since it is no longer an agreement between both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and is intended as the Parties’ final expression and complete and exclusive statement of the terms thereof, superseding all prior or contemporaneous agreem ents, representations, promises and understandings, whether written or oral. Neither Party is to be bound by any pre -printed terms appearing in the other Party’s form documents, tariffs, purchase orders, quotations, acknowledgments, invoices, or other ins truments. This Agreement may be amended or modified by an instrument signed by either Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments require a 'writing signed by both Parties'. The changed text removes the 'in writing' requirement and only requires a signature by 'either Party'. The contradicted text states that the agreement is the 'entire agreement and understanding of the Parties', and thus requires both parties to agree. By allowing either party to amend the agreement without both parties signing, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, allowing one party to alter the entire agreement without the consent of the other.
